channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Google Chrome adds infostealer protection against session cookie theft
item.description: Google has rolled out Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) protection in Chrome 146 for Windows, designed to block info-stealing malware from harvesting session cookies. [...]

item.title: The threat hunter’s gambit
item.description: Bill discusses why obsessing over strategy games is actually a secret weapon to outsmart threat actors.

item.title: Do Ceasefires Slow Cyberattacks? History Suggests Not
item.description: The cybersecurity community is waiting with bated breath to see if Iranian hackers will honor a ceasefire that doesn't actually name or directly involve them.

item.title: UAT-10362 Targets Taiwanese NGOs with LucidRook Malware in Spear-Phishing Campaigns
item.description: A previously undocumented threat cluster dubbed UAT-10362 has been attributed to spear-phishing campaigns targeting Taiwanese non-governmental organizations (NGOs) and suspected universities to deploy a new Lua-based...

item.title: Claude helps researcher dig up decade-old Apache ActiveMQ RCE vulnerability (CVE-2026-34197)
item.description: In the latest demonstration of how AI assistants can help with bug hunting, Horizon3.ai researcher Naveen Sunkavally used Claude to unearth CVE-2026-34197, a remote code execution vulnerability in Apache ActiveMQ that...

item.title: Mallory brings contextual threat intelligence to security operations
item.description: Mallory is launching an AI-native threat intelligence platform, purpose-built to answer the questions CISOs and their teams are asking every day: What are the real threat vectors for our organization? What’s actually...

item.title: Cloud Threats Retrospective 2026: What AI Changed (and What It Didn’t)
item.description: Insights from public incidents, cloud telemetry, and investigations into how cloud risk evolved in 2025

item.title: Streamline Compliance Reporting with Orca and Drata’s Integrated Vulnerability Management
item.description: We are excited to announce Orca now integrates with Drata, an Agentic Trust Management Platform, to offer comprehensive vulnerability assessments to identify compliance risks across your digital estate. Orca’s integra...

item.title: Google API Keys in Android Apps Expose Gemini Endpoints to Unauthorized Access
item.description: Dozens of such keys can be extracted from apps’ decompiled code to gain access to all Gemini endpoints. The post Google API Keys in Android Apps Expose Gemini Endpoints to Unauthorized Access appeared first on Securit...

item.title: Webinar: From noise to signal - What threat actors are targeting next
item.description: Threat actors often signal their intentions before launching attacks, from dark web chatter to access-broker listings and credential requests. Join our upcoming webinar with Flare Systems to learn how to turn those ea...

item.title: Bringing Security Visibility to Vercel with Wiz
item.description: Giving developers and security teams a shared view of application risk as it evolves.

item.title: OPSWAT adds predictive AI engine to MetaDefender for pre-execution threat detection
item.description: OPSWAT has announced OPSWAT Predictive Alin AI, its first proprietary AI-based threat detection engine for the MetaDefender Platform. This AI-based innovation introduces a new category of capability within the MetaDef...

item.title: Palo Alto Networks, SonicWall Patch High-Severity Vulnerabilities
item.description: The bugs could allow attackers to modify protected resources and escalate their privileges to administrator. The post Palo Alto Networks, SonicWall Patch High-Severity Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Acrobat Reader zero-day exploited in the wild for many months
item.description: Unknown attackers have exploited a zero-day Adobe Acrobat Reader vulnerability since November 2025 and possibly even earlier, security researcher Haifei Li has discovered. PDF files carry the exploit Haifei Li is one...

item.title: The Hidden Security Risks of Shadow AI in Enterprises
item.description: As AI tools become more accessible, employees are adopting them without formal approval from IT and security teams. While these tools may boost productivity, automate tasks, or fill gaps in existing workflows, they al...

item.title: Intruder expands cloud security with agentless container image scanning
item.description: Intruder has announced the release of Container Image Scanning, a new upgrade to its cloud security capabilities that automatically scans container images for vulnerabilities, granting customers actionable insight int...

item.title: Atomic Stealer MacOS ClickFix Attack Bypasses Apple Security Warnings
item.description: macOS 26.4 update introduced security warnings into Terminal to prevent ClickFix attacks, so attackers have shifted to Script Editor instead

item.title: Adobe Reader Zero-Day Exploited via Malicious PDFs Since December 2025
item.description: Threat actors have been exploiting a previously unknown zero-day vulnerability in Adobe Reader using maliciously crafted PDF documents since at least December 2025. The finding, detailed by EXPMON's Haifei Li, has bee...

item.title: The Hidden ROI of Visibility: Better Decisions, Better Behavior, Better Security
item.description: Beyond monitoring and compliance, visibility acts as a powerful deterrent, shaping user behavior, improving collaboration, and enabling more accurate, data-driven security decisions. The post The Hidden ROI of Visibil...

item.title: On Microsoft’s Lousy Cloud Security
item.description: ProPublica has a scoop : In late 2024, the federal government’s cybersecurity evaluators rendered a troubling verdict on one of Microsoft’s biggest cloud computing offerings. The tech giant’s “lack of proper detailed...

item.title: Middle East Hack-for-Hire Operation Traced to South Asian Cyber Espionage Group
item.description: A spear-phishing campaign which spread across the Middle East between 2023 and 2024 has now been linked to Bitter APT group

item.title: Bitter-Linked Hack-for-Hire Campaign Targets Journalists Across MENA Region
item.description: An apparent hack-for-hire campaign likely orchestrated by a threat actor with suspected ties to the Indian government targeted journalists, activists, and government officials across the Middle East and North Africa (...

item.title: Eurail says December data breach impacts 300,000 individuals
item.description: Eurail B.V., a European travel operator that provides digital passes covering 33 national railways, says attackers stole the personal information of over 300,000 individuals in a December 2025 data breach. [...]

item.title: Advenica’s File Scanner Kiosk scans USB media for malware
item.description: Advenica announced the File Scanner Kiosk, a system that scans USB media for malware and helps businesses reduce infection risk. With the reliance on external media for file transfers, organisations face increased vul...

item.title: Governance Gaps Emerge as AI Agents Drive 76% Increase in NHIs
item.description: SANS Institute reveals that AI agents are behind a 76% surge in non-human identities

item.title: Microsoft suspends dev accounts for high-profile open source projects
item.description: Microsoft has suspended developer accounts used to maintain multiple high-profile open-source projects without proper notification and no way to quickly reinstate them, effectively blocking them from publishing new so...

item.title: $3.6 Million Stolen in Bitcoin Depot Hack
item.description: A hacker transferred more than 50 bitcoin from the Bitcoin ATM operator’s wallets after stealing credentials. The post $3.6 Million Stolen in Bitcoin Depot Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Shaky Ceasefire Unlikely to Stop Cyberattacks From Iran-Linked Hackers for Long
item.description: Hackers vowed to revive its efforts against America when the time was right — demonstrating how digital warfare has become ingrained in military conflict. The post Shaky Ceasefire Unlikely to Stop Cyberattacks From Ir...

item.title: Russia's Forest Blizzard Nabs Rafts of Logins via SOHO Routers
item.description: Heard of fileless malware? How about malwareless cyber espionage? Russia's APT28 is spying on global organizations by modifying just one DNS setting in vulnerable routers.

item.title: Unpatchable Vulnerabilities of Kubernetes: CVE-2020-8562
item.description: A look at how Kubernetes CVE-2020-8562 allows attackers to bypass API server proxy protections using DNS rebinding

item.title: Elastic on Defence Cyber Marvel 2026: A Technical overview from the Exercise Floor
item.description: An overview of the Elastic Security and AI infrastructure deployed to support the UK Ministry of Defence's flagship cyber exercise, Defence Cyber Marvel 2026.

item.title: Cracks in the Bedrock: Agent God Mode
item.description: Unit 42 reveals "Agent God Mode" in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. Broad IAM permissions lead to privilege escalation and data exfiltration risks. The post Cracks in the Bedrock: Agent God Mode appeared first on Unit 42 .

item.title: Google: New UNC6783 hackers steal corporate Zendesk support tickets
item.description: A threat actor tracked as UNC6783 is compromising business process outsourcing (BPO) providers to gain access to high-value companies across multiple sectors. [...]

item.title: U.S. CISA adds a flaw in Ivanti EPMM to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds a flaw in Ivanti EPMM to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added a flaw...

item.title: Russia-linked APT28 uses PRISMEX to infiltrate Ukraine and allied infrastructure with advanced tactics
item.description: APT28 targets Ukraine and allies with PRISMEX malware, using stealthy techniques for espionage and command-and-control. Russia-linked group APT28 (aka UAC-0001, aka Fancy Bear, Pawn Storm, Sofacy Group, Sednit, BlueDe...

item.title: Threat Actors Get Crafty With Emojis to Escape Detection
item.description: When 🤖 means "bot available," 🧰 signifies "toolkit," or 💰💰💰 translates to "big ransom," bad actors can evade filters and keep it all on the down-low.

item.title: AI-Led Remediation Crisis Prompts HackerOne to Pause Bug Bounties
item.description: Discovery used to be the bottleneck for open source bugs, but with automated discovery, remediation's the bottleneck, which bounties don't fund.

item.title: Fraud Rockets Higher in Mobile-First Latin America
item.description: Cyber-fraudsters move quickly from compromised devices to account takeover to funds transfer, shifting money before many financial institutions can react.

item.title: Full Sail University to Open IBM Cyber Defense Range Powered by AWS and Cloud Range on Campus
Pluralsight Launches SecureReady to Help Organizations Build Job-Ready Cybersecurity Teams
Is a $30,000 GPU Good at Password Cracking?
item.description: A $30,000 AI GPU doesn't outperform consumer GPUs at password cracking. Specops explains why attackers don't need exotic hardware to break weak passwords. [...]

item.title: APT28 Deploys PRISMEX Malware in Campaign Targeting Ukraine and NATO Allies
item.description: The Russian threat actor known as APT28 (aka Forest Blizzard and Pawn Storm) has been linked to a fresh spear-phishing campaign targeting Ukraine and its allies to deploy a previously undocumented malware suite codena...

item.title: Iranian Threat Actors Disrupt US Critical Infrastructure via Exposed PLCs
item.description: Attackers compromised Internet-facing OT devices and caused file and display manipulation, operational disruption, and financial losses across sectors.

item.title: From bytecode to bytes: automated magic packet generation
item.description: By applying symbolic execution and the Z3 theorem prover to BPF bytecode, we’ve automated the generation of malware trigger packets, cutting analysis time from hours to seconds.

item.title: New Lua-based malware “LucidRook” observed in targeted attacks against Taiwanese organizations
item.description: Cisco Talos uncovered a cluster of activity we track as UAT-10362 conducting spear-phishing campaigns against Taiwanese non-governmental organizations (NGOs) and suspected universities to deliver a newly identified ma...

item.title: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Finds Thousands of Zero-Day Flaws Across Major Systems
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) company Anthropic announced a new cybersecurity initiative called Project Glasswing that will use a preview version of its new frontier model, Claude Mythos, to find and address security v...

item.title: Claude Discovers Apache ActiveMQ Bug Hidden for 13 Years
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude AI has helped researchers find a vulnerability in Apache ActiveMQ Classic

item.title: Iran‑Backed Threat Actors Hit US CNI Providers via Internet‑Facing OT Assets
item.description: CISA has revealed Iranian attacks causing disruption and financial loss at US critical infrastructure firms

item.title: N. Korean Hackers Spread 1,700 Malicious Packages Across npm, PyPI, Go, Rust
item.description: The North Korea-linked persistent campaign known as Contagious Interview has spread its tentacles by publishing malicious packages targeting the Go, Rust, and PHP ecosystems. "The threat actor's packages were designed...

item.title: U.S. agencies alert: Iran-linked actors target critical infrastructure PLCs
item.description: U.S. agencies warn Iran-linked threat actors are targeting internet-exposed PLCs used in critical infrastructure networks. U.S. agencies, including the FBI and CISA, warn that Iran-linked hackers are targeting interne...

item.title: How to use AI to manage cloud security threats
Cloudflare targets 2029 for full post-quantum security
item.description: Recent advances in quantum hardware and software have accelerated the timeline on which quantum attack might happen. Cloudflare is responding by moving our target for full post-quantum security to 2029.

item.title: Attackers exploit critical Flowise flaw CVE-2025-59528 for remote code execution
item.description: Attackers are exploiting a critical Flowise flaw, tracked as CVE-2025-59528 (CVSS score of 10), that lets them run malicious code and access systems due to poor validation of user-supplied JavaScript. Attackers are ac...

item.title: Storm-1175 Deploys Medusa Ransomware at 'High Velocity'
item.description: Microsoft says the financially motivated cybercrime group has exploited n-day and zero-day vulnerabilities in campaigns predicated on speed.

item.title: Grafana Patches AI Bug That Could Have Leaked User Data
item.description: By hiding malicious instructions on an attacker-controlled Web page, AI could ingest orders that appear benign but return sensitive data to the attacker's server.

item.title: Russian APT28 Hackers Hijack Routers to Steal Credentials, UK Security Agency Warns
item.description: Newly identified malicious campaigns are linked to virtual private servers modified by APT28 to operate as malicious DNS servers

item.title: Docker CVE-2026-34040 Lets Attackers Bypass Authorization and Gain Host Access
item.description: A high-severity security vulnerability has been disclosed in Docker Engine that could permit an attacker to bypass authorization plugins (AuthZ) under specific circumstances. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-340...

item.title: GPU Rowhammer Attack Enables Privilege Escalation and Full System Compromise
item.description: GPUBreach uses GPU Rowhammer on GDDR6 to flip bits, corrupt page tables and escalate to system root

item.title: RSAC 2026: How AI Is Reshaping Cybersecurity Faster Than Ever
item.description: Dark Reading's Kelly Jackson Higgins shares insights on the past, present, and future of cybersecurity after attending RSAC 2026 Conference.

item.title: Human vs. AI: Debates Shape RSAC 2026 Cybersecurity Trends
item.description: As AI dominated RSAC 2026, CISOs and industry leaders debated its role in security, from agentic applications to the challenges of scaling human involvement in decision-making.

item.title: Lies, Damned Lies, and Cybersecurity Metrics
item.description: A panel of five C-suite leaders discuss how cybersecurity success is measured and why it isn't improving results.

item.title: GrafanaGhost Exploit Bypasses AI Guardrails for Silent Data Exfiltration
item.description: GrafanaGhost chains AI prompt injection and URL flaws to exfiltrate sensitive Grafana data

item.title: Major outage cripples Russian banking apps and metro payments nationwide
item.description: A major outage hit Russian banking apps and payments, blocking card use, cash withdrawals, and mobile access for hours. A widespread outage disrupted banking apps and payment systems across Russia, leaving customers u...

item.title: Focusing on the People in Cybersecurity at RSAC 2026 Conference
item.description: AI dominated RSAC 2026 Conference, but it's still the humans in cybersecurity who matter most.

item.title: Over 1,000 Exposed ComfyUI Instances Targeted in Cryptomining Botnet Campaign
item.description: An active campaign has been observed targeting internet-exposed instances running ComfyUI, a popular stable diffusion platform, to enlist them into a cryptocurrency mining and proxy botnet. "A purpose-built Python sca...

item.title: The Trojan horse of cybercrime: Weaponizing SaaS notification pipelines
item.description: Cisco Talos has recently observed an increase in activity that is leveraging notification pipelines in popular collaboration platforms to deliver spam and phishing emails.

item.title: Hong Kong Police Can Force You to Reveal Your Encryption Keys
item.description: According to a new law, the Hong Kong police can demand that you reveal the encryption keys protecting your computer, phone, hard drives, etc.—even if you are just transiting the airport. In a security alert dated Mar...

item.title: Fortinet Releases Emergency Patch After FortiClient EMS Bug Is Exploited
item.description: Fortinet has updated its FortiClient EMS product after zero-day attacks surfaced

item.title: New GPUBreach Attack Enables Full CPU Privilege Escalation via GDDR6 Bit-Flips
item.description: New academic research has identified multiple RowHammer attacks against high-performance graphics processing units (GPUs) that could be exploited to escalate privileges and, in some cases, even take full control of a...

item.title: China-Linked Storm-1175 Exploits Zero-Days to Rapidly Deploy Medusa Ransomware
item.description: A China-based threat actor known for deploying Medusa ransomware has been linked to the weaponization of a combination of zero-day and N-day vulnerabilities to orchestrate "high-velocity" attacks and break into suscep...

item.title: AI-Assisted Supply Chain Attack Targets GitHub
item.description: PRT-scan is the second campaign in recent months where a threat actor appears to have leveraged AI for automated targeting of a widespread GitHub misconfiguration.

item.title: Axios Attack Shows How Complex Social Engineering Is Industrialized
item.description: The attack on the popular NPM package Axios is just one of many targeting maintainers and has shone a light on how threat actors can scale sophisticated social engineering campaigns.

item.title: Fortinet Issues Emergency Patch for FortiClient Zero-Day
item.description: The authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-35616, is the latest in a series of Fortinet vulnerabilities that have been exploited in the wild.

item.title: New Mexico’s Meta Ruling and Encryption
item.description: Mike Masnick points out that the recent New Mexico court ruling against Meta has some bad implications for end-to-end encryption, and security in general: If the “design choices create liability” framework seems worry...

item.title: Iran-Linked Password-Spraying Campaign Targets 300+ Israeli Microsoft 365 Organizations
item.description: An Iran-nexus threat actor is suspected to be behind a password-spraying campaign targeting Microsoft 365 environments in Israel and the U.A.E. amid ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The activity, assessed to be on...

item.title: Automated Credential Harvesting Campaign Exploits React2Shell Flaw
item.description: An emerging threat cluster tracked as UAT-10608 is exploiting vulnerable Web-exposed Next.js apps and using an automated tool to exfiltrate credentials, secrets, and other system data.

item.title: Shadow AI in Healthcare Is Here to Stay
item.description: Medical professionals are not going to stop using AI tools to manage growing workloads. Organizations should prioritize bolstering security protocols to limit their blast radius.

item.title: AI Is Entering Your Infrastructure. Now what?
item.description: The Tool Becomes the Operator AI is quietly moving from the chat window into the infrastructure itself. It is no longer just answering questions. It is writing code, interacting with APIs, responding to customers, and...

item.title: OWASP GenAI Security Project Gets Update, New Tools Matrix
item.description: In recognition of 21 generative AI risks, the standards group recommends that companies take separate but linked approaches to defending GenAI and agentic AI systems.

item.title: Multi-OS Cyberattacks: How SOCs Close a Critical Risk in 3 Steps
item.description: Your attack surface no longer lives on one operating system, and neither do the campaigns targeting it. In enterprise environments, attackers move across Windows endpoints, executive MacBooks, Linux infrastructure, an...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Axios Hack, Chrome 0-Day, Fortinet Exploits, Paragon Spyware and More
item.description: This week had real hits. The key software got tampered with. Active bugs showed up in the tools people use every day. Some attacks didn’t even need much effort because the path was already there. One weak spot now spr...

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-35616 - Fortinet FortiClient EMS Improper Access Control Vulnerability Thi...

item.title: How often are redirects used in phishing in 2026?, (Mon, Apr 6th)
item.description: In one of his recent diaries, Johannes discussed how open redirects are actively being sought out by threat actors[1], which made me wonder about how commonly these mechanisms are actually misusedâ€¦

item.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
item.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin head...

item.title: Security briefing: March 2026
$285 Million Drift Hack Traced to Six-Month DPRK Social Engineering Operation
item.description: Drift has revealed that the April 1, 2026, attack that led to the theft of $285 million was the culmination of a months-long targeted and meticulously planned social engineering operation undertaken by the Democratic...

item.title: Elastic Security Integrations Roundup: Q1 2026
item.description: Elastic Security Labs announces nine new integrations for Elastic Security spanning cloud security, endpoint visibility, email threat detection, identity and SIEM.

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Jurassic Fish Chokes on Squid
item.description: Here’s a fossil of a 150-million year old fish that choked to death on a belemnite rostrum : the hard, internal shell of an extinct, squid-like animal. Original paper . As usual, you can also use this squid post to ta...

item.title: Apple Breaks Precedent, Patches DarkSword for iOS 18
item.description: Even organizations with users unwilling or unable to adopt iOS 26 can now protect themselves from a severe mobile OS-cracking tool.

item.title: Blast Radius of TeamPCP Attacks Expands Amid Hacker Infighting
item.description: As organizations disclose breaches tied to TeamPCP's supply chain attacks, ShinyHunters and Lapsus$ are getting involved, taking credit, and creating a murky situation for enterprises.

item.title: Picking Up 'Skull Vibrations'? Could Be XR Headset Authentication
item.description: "Skull vibration harmonics generated by vital signs" can be used to sign in to VR, AR, and MR headsets, according to emerging research.

item.title: Claude Source Code Leak Highlights Big Supply Chain Missteps
item.description: Or, why the software supply chain should be treated as critical infrastructure with guardrails built in at every layer.

item.title: Chainguard Unveils Factory 2.0 to Automate Hardening the Software Supply Chain
item.description: The rebuilt Chainguard platform adds deeper security designed to continuously reconcile open source artifacts across containers, libraries, agent skills, and GitHub Actions.

item.title: Orca Security Featured in SACR’s 2026 Unified Agentic Defense Platforms Report
item.description: Software Analyst Cyber Research (SACR) has published its report, The Convergence of AI and Data Security: An Industry-Wide Technoscope of Unified Agentic Defense Platforms. In this evaluation of 15 leading vendors sha...

item.title: CrowdStrike Next-Gen SIEM Can Now Ingest Microsoft Defender Telemetry
item.description: Once CrowdStrike's nemesis, Microsoft is now a collaborator. A shared interest in Formula 1 helped thaw the years-long fierce rivalry.

item.title: New SparkCat Variant in iOS, Android Apps Steals Crypto Wallet Recovery Phrase Images
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new version of the SparkCat malware on the Apple App Store and Google Play Store, more than a year after the trojan was discovered targeting both the mobile operating system...

item.title: Drift Loses $285 Million in Durable Nonce Social Engineering Attack Linked to DPRK
item.description: Solana-based decentralized exchange Drift has confirmed that attackers drained about $285 million from the platform during a security incident that took place on April 1, 2026. "Earlier today, a malicious actor gained...

item.title: New Phishing Platform Used in Credential Theft Campaigns Against C-Suite Execs
item.description: A large-scale credential theft campaign targeting senior executives has been linked to a previously unknown automated phishing platform called Venom

item.title: Geopolitics, AI, and Cybersecurity: Insights From RSAC 2026
item.description: AI-driven threats, global leadership shifts, and the future of cybersecurity in a rapidly evolving landscape were among the discussions at RSAC 2026 Conference.

item.title: Four security principles for agentic AI systems
item.description: Agentic AI represents a qualitative shift in how software operates. Traditional software executes deterministic instructions. Generative AI responds to human prompts with output that humans review and use at their dis...

item.title: Hackers Exploit CVE-2025-55182 to Breach 766 Next.js Hosts, Steal Credentials
item.description: A large-scale credential harvesting operation has been observed exploiting the React2Shell vulnerability as an initial infection vector to steal database credentials, SSH private keys, Amazon Web Services (AWS) secret...

item.title: Security Bosses Are All in on AI: Here's Why
item.description: CISOs are bullish on AI and have big plans to roll out future tools. We talk to Reddit CISO Frederick Lee and leading Omdia analyst Dave Gruber about how AI is working out in the real world, as well as its future prom...

item.title: RSAC 2026: AI Dominates, But Community Remains Key to Security
item.description: As AI took center stage at this year's conference, experts debated automation, oversight and the evolving role of human intelligence in cybersecurity — despite the US government's notable absence.

item.title: New 'Storm' Infostealer Remotely Decrypts Stolen Credentials
item.description: This modern infostealer adopted server-side decryption of stolen credentials to bypass security controls

item.title: NCSC Issues Security Alert Over Hackers Targeting WhatsApp and Signal Accounts
item.description: The UK’s cybersecurity agency offered advice to “high-risk’ individuals” on how to protect against social engineering and cyber-attacks

item.title: vSphere and BRICKSTORM Malware: A Defender's Guide
item.description: Written by: Stuart Carrera Introduction Building on recent BRICKSTORM research from Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG), this post explores the evolving threats facing virtualized environments. These operations di...

item.title: Apple Expands iOS 18 Security Updates Amid DarkSword Threat
item.description: iOS/iPadOS 18.7.7 updates expanded to protect older devices from DarkSword web exploit kit

item.title: [Video] The TTP Ep 21: When Attackers Become Trusted Users
item.description: An episode of the Talos Threat Perspective on the 2025 Year in Review trends. We explore how identity is being used to gain, extend, and maintain access inside environments.

item.title: Why we're rethinking cache for the AI era
item.description: The explosion of AI-bot traffic, representing over 10 billion requests per week, has opened up new challenges and opportunities for cache design. We look at some of the ways AI bot traffic differs from humans, how thi...

item.title: Researchers Observe Sub-One-Hour Ransomware Attacks
item.description: Halcyon says Akira is now capable of carrying out an entire ransomware attack in less than an hour

item.title: GitHub Used as Covert Channel in Multi-Stage Malware Campaign
item.description: LNK files use GitHub C2, embedded decoders and PowerShell for persistence and data exfiltration

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Pre-Auth Chains, Android Rootkits, CloudTrail Evasion & 10 More Stories
item.description: The latest ThreatsDay Bulletin is basically a cheat sheet for everything breaking on the internet right now. No corporate fluff or boring lectures here, just a quick and honest look at the messy reality of keeping sys...

item.title: UAT-10608: Inside a large-scale automated credential harvesting operation targeting web applications
item.description: Talos is disclosing a large-scale automated credential harvesting campaign carried out by a threat cluster we currently track as UAT-10608. The campaign is primarily leveraging a collection framework dubbed “NEXUS Lis...

item.title: Qilin EDR killer infection chain
item.description: This blog provides an in-depth analysis of the malicious “msimg32.dll” used in Qilin ransomware attacks, which is a multi-stage infection chain targeting EDR systems.

item.title: Inside the Talos 2025 Year in Review: A discussion on what the data means for defenders
item.description: A conversation between Cisco Talos and Cisco Security leaders on the 2025 threat landscape, from identity attacks and legacy vulnerabilities to AI-driven threats, and what defenders should prioritize now.

item.title: An overview of ransomware threats in Japan in 2025 and early detection insights from Qilin cases
item.description: There were 134 ransomware incidents reported in Japan in 2025, representing a 17.5% year-over-year increase from 2024.

item.title: Apple Expands iOS 18.7.7 Update to More Devices to Block DarkSword Exploit
item.description: Apple on Wednesday expanded the availability of iOS 18.7.7 and iPadOS 18.7.7 to a broader range of devices to protect users from the risk posed by a recently disclosed exploit kit known as DarkSword. "We enabled the a...

item.title: The Sysdig MCP server is now available in AWS Marketplace
item.description: Correlating security signals from cloud services, container registries, and runtime environments is an important component of maintaining secure cloud deployments. But making this a seamless process, without the need...

item.title: Risk isn’t reduced until you take action: How teams resolve issues in the cloud
item.description: Effective remediation depends on real-time insight that allows security teams to quickly investigate, understand, and respond to threats with precision. By taking targeted actions like stopping compromised workloads,...

item.title: Introducing Programmable Flow Protection: custom DDoS mitigation logic for Magic Transit customers
item.description: Magic Transit customers can now program their own DDoS mitigation logic and deploy it across Cloudflare’s global network. This enables precise, stateful mitigation for custom and proprietary UDP protocols.
